content: Per-tenant encryption with AWS KMS: Create dedicated customer managed keys per tenant for data-at-rest encryption. Enforce tenant isolation via key policies preventing cross-tenant key usage.

name: skill-marketplace-security-pipeline
description: 7-stage security pipeline for skill marketplace (learned from ClawHavoc): static analysis, dependency audit (OSV), sandbox testing (OpenSandbox MicroVM), permission validation, Ed25519 dual-signing (author+platform), runtime monitoring config, community reporting. 5-tier trust model with Cedar policy enforcement. Prevents prompt injection, credential theft, supply chain attacks.

content: 7-stage skill security pipeline: (1) Static analysis (AST scanning), (2) Dependency audit (OSV database), (3) Sandbox run (OpenSandbox MicroVM), (4) Permission validation (declared vs actual), (5) Cryptographic signing (Ed25519 dual-signature), (6) Runtime monitoring config, (7) Community reporting. Prevents ClawHavoc-style attacks.

description: ClawHavoc supply chain attack: 1,184 malicious skills (12% of ClawHub registry)
resolution: 7-stage security pipeline with static analysis, sandbox testing, and cryptographic signing before publish

name: dynamodb-leadingkeys-gsi-isolation
description: DynamoDB LeadingKeys condition with ForAllValues:StringNotLike prevents GSI cross-tenant data leakage: Deny policy blocks queries where not all accessed partition keys match TENANT#{id}* pattern

name: iam-permission-boundaries-escalation-prevention
description: IAM permission boundaries prevent privilege escalation: Even if agent role has iam:CreateRole, boundary limits new roles to approved actions, blocking IAM/billing/org modifications

name: cross-account-role-external-id
description: CrossAccountRoleManager enforces external ID validation (minimum 16 characters) for all cross-account role assumptions to prevent confused deputy attacks. Credential chaining: tenant credentials -> STS AssumeRole -> cross-account credentials with session tags for audit trail

name: gsi-tenant-filterexpression-enforcement
description: All DynamoDB GSI queries must include FilterExpression='tenantId = :tenantId' to prevent cross-tenant data leakage. GSIs create new partition keys that don't inherit base table's tenant isolation. Applied to skills/registry.ts (searchSkills, listByCategory, listByTrustLevel) and propagated through skills/discovery.ts call chain.

name: openclaw-exec-approval-system
description: OpenClaw exec approval has 3 knobs: Security (deny/allowlist/full), Ask (off/on-miss/always), Ask Fallback (deny when UI unavailable). Approval shows command, args, CWD, agent ID. Options: Allow once/Always allow/Deny. Chimera equivalent: Cedar policy evaluation + human-in-the-loop for high-risk operations.

name: skill-registry-tenant-isolation
description: All DynamoDB queries in SkillRegistry enforce multi-tenant isolation via FilterExpression='tenantId = :tenantId' parameter. GSI queries (listByCategory, listByTrustLevel) MUST include FilterExpression even though PK is category/trust_level - without this, GSI queries leak data across tenants. Scan operations require tenantId in FilterExpression. This prevents ClawHavoc-style cross-tenant data leakage attacks.

name: cedar-trust-engine-authorization
description: SkillTrustEngine implements Cedar-based runtime authorization with 5-tier trust model: (1) Platform skills have unrestricted access, (2) Verified skills enforce declared permissions, (3) Community skills limited to /tmp filesystem only, no network/shell/memory/secrets, (4) Private skills follow verified constraints, (5) Experimental skills strictest - /tmp only, no elevated permissions. Each action type (file_read, file_write, network_access, run_shell, read_memory, write_memory, read_secret, invoke_tool) has dedicated authorizer method that checks trust level + declared permissions.

content: cedar-authorization-cross-tenant-isolation: Cedar policy engine enforces cross-tenant isolation via forbid policies that take precedence over permits. cross-tenant-isolation policy: forbid all actions where context.tenantId != resource.tenantId. Default deny (no matching policy = deny). Suspended tenants denied via forbid policy on tenantStatus. Trial tier restrictions enforced via tier-based forbid policies.

name: token-bucket-rate-limiting
description: RateLimiter from @chimera/core implements token bucket algorithm with DynamoDB-backed state. Per-tenant rate limits prevent cross-tenant DoS. Atomic updates with ConditionExpression prevent race conditions. 5-minute sliding windows track metrics with TTL for automatic cleanup.

content: cross-tenant-isolation-testing: 7-dimension isolation tests: session, rate limit, cost tracking, quota, Cedar policy, memory namespace, header injection resistance. Tests at packages/chat-gateway/src/__tests__/cross-tenant-isolation.test.ts.

gsi-cross-tenant-filter-enforcement: GSI queries MUST include FilterExpression for tenantId even when tenantId is in the query parameters. GSI secondary indexes don't enforce partition isolation, so cross-tenant leakage occurs without FilterExpression. Pattern: KeyConditionExpression + FilterExpression='tenantId = :tenantId'

fail-closed-rate-limiting: Rate limiters MUST fail closed on errors (return {allowed: false}) rather than throwing exceptions. Wrap checkRateLimit in try-catch and return denial on any error (DynamoDB unavailable, unexpected errors). Prevents fail-open vulnerability where exceptions might allow unrestricted access.
